time-dilation.title: New Tripoli time dilation (subjective-year multiplier)
time-dilation.description: Computes subjective-years-lived multipliers for a given New Tripoli time-dilation rate (subjective years per real calendar year) and Universal Sabbath reset interval.

kinetic-probe.title: New Tripoli kinetic probe (relativistic delivery + deceleration lottery)
kinetic-probe.description: Computes the cost and survival of an ETI kinetic-probe delivery: travel time, relativistic cruise kinetic energy (γ−1)mc² (with the classical ½mv² reported alongside), and the vaporization margin that gates the deceleration lottery.
citations: Canon - New Tripoli.md (ETI kinetic-probe delivery / probe block)

feasibility.title: New Tripoli vat feasibility (Landauer digital-mind pricing vs Sahara cap)
feasibility.description: Computes the total power draw of a New Tripoli population split between biological brains (20 W × acceleration × support overhead) and digital minds (Landauer-priced at 10^fidelity irreversible bit-ops/s), then compares it to the Sahara solar cap (~230 TW).
citations: Cognitive Husbandry.md — Technical Feasibility (Brain in a Vat)

acceleration-ceiling.title: New Tripoli acceleration ceiling by augmentation stage
acceleration-ceiling.description: Computes the subjective-time acceleration ceiling for a given New Tripoli augmentation stage (vat / sensory / metabolic / synaptic / hybrid / upload), returning the floor, ceiling, and midpoint of that stage's plausible acceleration range, the biological thermal load at the midpoint (20 W × acceleration; null once the stage is non-biological, above 1000×), the subjective years lived at the midpoint over a given wall-clock span, and the stage's named bottleneck.

comms-lag.title: New Tripoli comms lag between subjective-time rates
comms-lag.description: Computes round-trip communications lag between two parties running at different subjective-time acceleration rates, given a wall-clock speed-of-light latency.
citations: Canon - New Tripoli.md §30 (New Centauri — contact-list problem)

ring-density.title: New Tripoli orbital ring housing density
ring-density.description: Computes housing capacity of the New Tripoli orbital ring plus core habitat, given ring depth, per-person area allowance, floor count, and core population.
citations: Canon - New Tripoli.md §5 (ring & housing geometry / population)

birthday-sacrifice.title: New Tripoli birthday-sacrifice subjective time cost
birthday-sacrifice.description: Computes the subjective time you and a family member accrue under different simulated dilation rates over a given calendar span, plus the per-year subjective-time cost of the gap and the equivalent number of 6-month Tripoli blocks that cost represents.
citations: Canon - New Tripoli.md §18 (Time Dilation / doubling schedules)

synthetic-body.title: New Tripoli synthetic body mass
synthetic-body.description: Computes the mass (kg and lb), human-mass ratio, and water-buoyancy of a synthetic body skeleton built from a given material, scaled from the canon osmium reference build.
citations: Canon - New Tripoli.md §35/§37 (synthetic body / osmium skeleton)

selection-sorter.title: New Tripoli selection cost
selection-sorter.description: Computes how many of the 8.1B canon population are excluded (and how many remain) under a named selection criterion for who is offered the mind-upload path (e.g. literacy, wealth, longevity).

interface-bandwidth.title: New Tripoli brain-interface bandwidth gap
interface-bandwidth.description: Computes how far a brain-computer interface of a given channel count is from whole-brain bandwidth (~86 billion neurons).
citations: Feasibility Audit §4.4 (interface bandwidth / the write problem)
Koch, K. et al. (2006), Current Biology 16(14):1428–1434
Neuralink PRIME study (2024+) — N1, 1,024 electrodes / 64 threads

tech-tree.title: New Tripoli post-Wake tech-tree path solver
tech-tree.description: Solves the post-Wake reindustrialization dependency graph (35 nodes, tiers T0–T9, ending at a femtoscale assembler).
citations: Canon - New Tripoli.md §36 (post-Wake reindustrialization of New Anasis)

about.title: New Tripoli chaingraph provenance manifest
about.description: Emits a tamper-evident ChainGraph provenance manifest for an arbitrary sim run: echoes the declared inputs and canon citations, optionally threads to a parent artifact's execution_hash, and reports input/citation counts and parent linkage.

feasibility-crosswalk.title: New Tripoli feasibility crosswalk (graded D1–D10 claim ledger)
feasibility-crosswalk.description: Grades the ten load-bearing feasibility claims (C-D1..C-D10) of the New Tripoli scenario against a single scenario config: delegates to the five physics kernels (kinetic probe, time dilation, vat feasibility, interface bandwidth, comms lag), reads their decision pointers, and emits a ledger of {claim, verdict, driver} plus verdict tallies and the binding (worst) verdict.

war-finance-default.title: Alt-history war-finance default (treasury drain → default year → act-of-war clause)
war-finance-default.description: Computes an accounting identity over stipulated war finances: a HORIZON-bounded year loop drains reserves by (war cost + debt interest − revenue), financing each deficit with debt, and reports the first year reserves fall below the default threshold, the default calendar year, terminal reserves, compounded debt, and whether an act-of-war clause is tripped.
citations: Alt History - What if WW1 had been avoided.md (Big Six war-finance thread)

nuclear-program-clock.title: Alt-history nuclear program clock (enrichment → first-device date + detection signature)
nuclear-program-clock.description: Computes a program timeline from stipulated fissile inputs: time-to-critical-mass plus a fixed engineering lead gives the first-device calendar year, and the standard yield→body-wave-magnitude relation (mb = 4.0 + 0.75·log10 kt) plus test medium gives the detection signature.
citations: Alt History - The Undisclosed Program.md (program-clock thread)
Soviet RDS-1 test 1949 + Vela/AFTAC detection record (calibration)

attribution-decay.title: Alt-history attribution decay (evidence age + censorship → attribution-confidence curve)
attribution-decay.description: Computes an attribution-confidence curve from stipulated inputs: an initial confidence decays geometrically per year (accelerated by a censorship factor), while corroborating tests pull it back toward certainty, and a bounded scan reports the year confidence first crosses the deniability threshold.
citations: Alt History - The Undisclosed Program.md (attribution thread)

injustice-ledger.title: Alt-history injustice ledger (branch tolls → unstable-ranking verdict)
injustice-ledger.description: Scores each alt-history branch two ways — direct battle-deaths only, and full-weighted (adding indirect deaths, displacement, and lost sovereignty) — then compares the least-unjust leaders.
citations: Geneva Declaration Global Burden of Armed Violence — indirect:direct band (calibration)

stadium-capacity.title: Stadium-capacity geometry (population/irradiance → footprint, fits_single_stadium)
stadium-capacity.description: Sizes the New Tripoli "single stadium civilization" image: physical substrate footprint (population × per-mind area) against a large-stadium footprint, alongside the solar-panel area the matching power supply would need (population × per-mind watts ÷ latitude irradiance).

intake-to-arc.title: Intake to arc
intake-to-arc.description: The whole scenario as a 4-step delivery→power→dilation→thermal pipeline; no gate, terminal verdict = weakest link.

steps.handoff: Export the probe result, then open step 2 (power).
Export the power result, then open step 3 (dilation).
Export the dilation result, then open step 4 (thermal).
Final step — export the Policy Mandate for your audit trail.

deceleration-lottery.title: Deceleration lottery
deceleration-lottery.description: The D1 delivery fix as a fast-fail decision: gates on the kinetic probe's own /branch label (vaporized/destroyed → end; survives → anchor provenance).

steps.handoff: If the probe survives (B_survives), export and open step 2 to anchor provenance. A vaporized/destroyed branch ends the chain here.
Final step — export the Policy Mandate for your audit trail.

substrate-decision.title: Substrate decision
substrate-decision.description: The D10 → D7 hinge: gates on time dilation's /upload_required flag (biological holds → end; upload required → anchor provenance).

steps.handoff: If upload_required is true, export and open step 2 to anchor provenance. A_biological_holds ends the chain here.
Final step — export the Policy Mandate for your audit trail.

energy-envelope.title: Energy envelope
energy-envelope.description: "Can it actually run": gates on vat feasibility's /sahara_pct (over capacity → end, binding constraint = power; feasible → interface, then thermal).

steps.handoff: If Sahara capacity is exceeded, the chain ends here (binding constraint = power). Otherwise export and open step 2 (interface).
Export the interface result, then open step 3 (thermal).
Final step — export the Policy Mandate for your audit trail.

friendship-across-tiers.title: Friendship across tiers
friendship-across-tiers.description: The physics of friendship: two dilated parties running at different subjective-time rates, plus the comms budget across the gap.

steps.handoff: Export your dilation result, then open step 2 for the other party.
Export their dilation result, then open step 3 (comms lag).
Final step — export the Policy Mandate for your audit trail.

provenance-anchor.title: Provenance anchor
provenance-anchor.description: The OCG backbone every other chain terminates into: any tool's run, threaded to a provenance-manifest anchor.

steps.handoff: Export the source result, then open step 2 to anchor provenance.
Final step — export the Policy Mandate for your audit trail.

feasibility-audit-crosswalk.title: Feasibility audit crosswalk
feasibility-audit-crosswalk.description: The meta fan-in: grades all C-D1..C-D10 claims against a single scenario config, then anchors provenance unless the binding verdict is Barred.

steps.handoff: If the binding_verdict is Barred, the chain ends here (do not anchor a "passing" run). Otherwise export and open step 2 to anchor provenance.
Final step — export the Policy Mandate for your audit trail.

structural-rhyme.title: Structural rhyme
structural-rhyme.description: The partial-hash "provable rhyme": the same clock+decay sub-chain under Actor A then Actor B, run as one 4-step chain with no gate. Because a step's hash does not fold in its parent, identical knobs → identical per-step hashes — the rhyme is a hash identity, not a claim.

steps.handoff: Actor A's program clock — export it, then open step 2 (Actor A attribution decay).
Actor A's attribution decay — export it, then open step 3 (Actor B program clock).
Actor B's program clock — its execution hash equals step 1's iff the clock knobs match. Export it, then open step 4.
Final step — Actor B's attribution decay; its hash equals step 2's iff the decay knobs match. Export the Policy Mandate for your audit trail.

injustice-conservation.title: Injustice conservation
injustice-conservation.description: The capstone meta fan-in: grade every conflict branch in one ledger call, then anchor the hashed ledger. The thesis is that the least-unjust ranking is unstable under defensible counting rules — the accounting is the exposed, adjustable argument.

steps.handoff: Grades all branches and returns the unstable-ranking verdict. Export it, then open step 2 to anchor provenance.
Final step — export the Policy Mandate for your audit trail.
